rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is dynamic, within-interaction frontline cue effects on immediate CSAT in service-failure encounters, whereas the cited paper models satisfaction-to-repurchase behavior/intent thresholds and response bias in automotive customers. With only a DOI match and no in-text citation context, the evidence shows thematic overlap on “satisfaction” but not clear substantive use (e.g., method, mechanism, or direct comparison).
